stocks.recent_news: Stock at Rs 2,299.50 on April 24, 2026; m-cap Rs 64,892 Cr. Q3 FY26 (Dec 2025) net profit Rs 403.21 Cr (+15.88% YoY) on revenue Rs 3,888 Cr. MarketsMOJO 'Strong Buy' rating reaffirmed April 8, 2026. Stock up 3-6% on April 23 in Nifty Pharma rally. Hengrui licensing deal (Sept 2025): Glenmark received exclusive rights to trastuzumab rezetecan with $18M upfront; EU marketing authorisation submissions for Zeqmelit expected H2 2026. Co-launched generic semaglutide in India alongside Sun Pharma and Dr Reddy's in March 2026. Profits up 890% over trailing year, stock up 66% YoY.
stocks.sector_context: Nifty Pharma rallied 3% to 23,164 on April 23, 2026 with broad-based participation. Indian Pharma Market (IPM) grew 10.7% YoY in March 2026, fourth consecutive month of double-digit growth. US tariff overhang remains key risk for export-heavy pharma; analyst preference is shifting toward domestically-focused names (Sun Pharma, Dr Reddy's, Eris). Generic semaglutide launch is a sector-wide India growth catalyst post-patent expiry.
stocks.peer_comparison: Glenmark participated in April 23 sector rally (3-6% gain) but underperformed Piramal Pharma (+9%) and Dr Reddy's (+8%). Trailing 12-month return of +66% is strong vs peers. Profit growth (+890% YoY) is the steepest in pharma but partly off a low base. Analyst preference list (Sun, Dr Reddy's, Eris) places Glenmark in second tier for tariff defensiveness.
red_flags: US tariff exposure (26% reciprocal tariff announced April 2, 2026) is a sector overhang; Glenmark's US generics business is exposed
Stock has run hard YTD/YoY - profit-booking risk into Q4 results
Margin trajectory will be scrutinized after one-off gains in prior quarters
catalysts: Q4 FY26 results and final dividend expected May 2026 (analysts model Rs 5-8/share)
EU marketing authorisation submission for Zeqmelit (trastuzumab rezetecan) in H2 2026
FY27 guidance and margin commentary at Q4 earnings call
Continued Nifty Pharma sector rotation as defensive trade vs cyclicals

stocks.recent_news: Stock at Rs 979.35 on April 24, 2026; m-cap Rs 62,494 Cr. Q4 FY26 results: net income Rs 385 Cr (+29% YoY, beat consensus Rs 354 Cr); revenue Rs 739 Cr (+30% YoY, beat Rs 708 Cr). FY26 PAT Rs 1,529.38 Cr. Final dividend Rs 12.50/share approved; AGM scheduled July 8, 2026. Board met April 27, 2026. Hit fresh 52W high in April 2026. Analyst ratings: 21 Buy / 3 Hold / 1 Sell.
stocks.sector_context: Indian AMC sector strongly bid on record mutual fund inflows. SIP contributions hit record Rs 32,000 Cr in March 2026; industry AUM at Rs 73.73 lakh Cr at end-March. Nomura projects NAM-INDIA AUM CAGR of 21% over FY25-28 (vs HDFC AMC 19%). AMC stocks (HDFC AMC, Nippon, ICICI Pru) rallied up to 5-6% across April. SEBI scrutiny on mutual fund expense ratios remains a structural sector risk.
stocks.peer_comparison: NAM-INDIA's Q4 net income growth (+29% YoY) outpaced HDFC AMC's FY26 PAT trajectory (HDFC AMC FY26 PAT Rs 2,858 Cr is larger absolute but growth profile broadly comparable). Kotak downgraded NAM to 'ADD' from 'BUY' citing HDFC AMC's superior earnings outlook. Nomura calls both top picks. NAM has the higher projected AUM CAGR but lower absolute scale.
red_flags: Stock at fresh 52W high - technical risk of profit-taking
Kotak downgrade from BUY to ADD signals relative-value caution vs HDFC AMC
Sensitivity to equity market correction - AUM-linked revenue is procyclical
Regulatory risk: SEBI continuing review of TER (Total Expense Ratio) framework
catalysts: Final dividend Rs 12.50/share record date and AGM July 8, 2026
April monthly AUM/SIP data release in early May (sector-level catalyst)
FY27 guidance post Q4 already delivered - flow data is the live catalyst
Nomura/sell-side reiteration of top-pick status

stocks.recent_news: Stock at Rs 188.02 on April 27, 2026, near 52W high of Rs 187.60 (52W low Rs 138.12). Signed 25-year PPAs for 600 MW wind-solar hybrid at Rs 3.74-3.75/kWh on April 16, 2026 - significant renewable capacity addition. Q4 FY26 board meeting expected May 2026; Street estimates PAT Rs 494.50 Cr (+32.57% YoY) on revenue Rs 4,389 Cr (+13.21% YoY). Listed by Nuvama and Business Standard as a top pick in the power sector ahead of Q4. 12-month consensus target Rs 175-200 (some near current price after run-up).
stocks.sector_context: Hotter-than-normal summer 2026 driving peak demand to 275-285 GW (forecast +8-12% YoY). Tata Power, Suzlon, NTPC and CESC seen as key Q4 beneficiaries. India targeting 500 GW non-fossil capacity by 2030 - renewable PPAs structurally re-rating utilities. Headwind: 26% US reciprocal tariff (April 2, 2026) triggered FII outflows from Indian equities including CESC. Adani Power, JSW Energy, Tata Power outperformed Nifty by 10-25 ppt YTD 2026.
stocks.peer_comparison: CESC has caught up sharply, now trading at/near 52W high after lagging earlier in April. PPA win shifts CESC narrative toward renewables, closing the gap with Tata Power and NTPC stories. Q4 PAT growth of +32.57% YoY (consensus) would be best-in-class among regulated utilities. Analyst preference still tilted to Tata Power/NTPC for scale, but CESC's renewable acceleration is a re-rating catalyst.
red_flags: Stock at fresh 52W high - run-up may have priced in Q4 beat
FII selling pressure from US tariff overhang affecting power sector beta
Regulated business model caps RoE at ~15.5% on approved capital
Renewable PPA tariffs (Rs 3.74-3.75/kWh) are competitive but margin-thin
catalysts: Q4 FY26 results and final dividend expected May 2026 (consensus PAT +32% YoY)
Summer 2026 peak demand 275-285 GW supports volumes through Q1 FY27
Execution milestones on 600 MW wind-solar hybrid PPAs
Potential FY27 capex guidance with renewable acceleration

stocks.recent_news: Stock at Rs 318.50 on April 27, 2026; m-cap Rs 41,474 Cr. Hit fresh 52W high April 16, 2026 with intraday +10.92% on heavy institutional volumes (turnover Rs 474 Cr). Exchange sought clarification April 17; NLC confirmed surge was market-driven with no undisclosed announcement. Won composite licences for Semhardih and Raipura Phosphorite-Limestone blocks in Balod district, Chhattisgarh (Tranche-V critical & strategic minerals auction). Signed EUR 100 million loan agreement with KfW on April 20, 2026 to fund solar and BESS projects. February 2026 MoU with NALCO for 1,200 MW thermal/renewable collaboration.
stocks.sector_context: PSU power utilities in re-rating mode driven by (a) summer peak demand 275-285 GW, (b) GoI critical minerals strategy, (c) green capex push. NLC targets 10 GW renewable capacity by 2030 (currently ~1.4 GW). KfW concessional financing reinforces renewable execution. Lignite/coal generation still benefits from heatwave-driven dispatch. PSU re-rating cycle broadly supportive (PFC, REC, NLC, NTPC all bid).
stocks.peer_comparison: NLC outperformed sector and broader market on April 16 with sharp intraday move. Critical minerals diversification (phosphorite-limestone) differentiates NLC from pure-play lignite/coal peers like Coal India. Smaller scale than Coal India / NTPC but commanding premium attention via diversification narrative and KfW financing milestone.
red_flags: Stock at fresh 52W high with concentrated April rally - profit-booking risk
April 16 volume spike required NSE clarification - retail/momentum-driven volatility
Critical minerals revenue is exploration-stage, not commercial; multi-year monetisation
Lignite/coal exposure faces long-term ESG and energy-transition pressure
catalysts: Q4 FY26 results and final dividend (Rs 3-4/share expected) in May 2026; interim Rs 3.60 already paid
Summer 2026 peak demand window
KfW EUR 100M loan deployment milestones for solar/BESS projects
Critical minerals exploration progress in Chhattisgarh blocks
1,200 MW NALCO thermal/renewable JV milestones

stocks.recent_news: Stock at Rs 970-975 on April 22, 2026 (vs Rs 934.85 on April 13). Q3 FY26 results: revenue Rs 1,908 Cr, EBITDA Rs 345 Cr (18% margin), PAT Rs 115 Cr - YoY growth across all metrics. Net debt cut sharply to Rs 365 Cr from Rs 14,304 lakh in FY23. Industry FY26 all-India gross box office hit record Rs 13,400 Cr (+13% YoY). Capital-light expansion strategy: 100-110 new screens annually under FOCO model, capex Rs 350-400 Cr. Premium-format occupancy 35-40% vs overall 28-30%. Q4 FY26 results expected April-May 2026.
stocks.sector_context: Indian theatrical exhibition at record collections; FY27 starts with strong content slate (8+ films expected to cross Rs 100 Cr in Q1 FY27). Dhurandhar: The Revenge boosting Q4 FY26 and early FY27 momentum. Hindi box office at record, regional cinema expanding, Hollywood recovering. IPL 2026 schedule overlaps Q1 FY27 - traditional headwind for screen footfalls. South India contributes ~40% of PVR INOX screen mix.
stocks.peer_comparison: PVR INOX is the dominant listed multiplex (combined post-2023 merger). No direct listed peer of comparable scale; competes with single-screen and unorganized exhibitors plus OTT. Capital-light expansion mirrors Inox Leisure's pre-merger playbook. Premium screen mix and net-debt reduction are sector-best metrics.
red_flags: IPL 2026 schedule competes for footfalls in Q1 FY27
Heavy dependence on Bollywood content cycle - flop run can quickly compress occupancy
OTT continues to compress theatrical windows
Margin sensitivity to F&B volumes; ticket-price elasticity in Tier 2/3 markets
catalysts: Q4 FY26 results expected April-May 2026 with final dividend (~Rs 2/share)
FY27 Q1 strong film slate (8+ Rs 100 Cr+ openers expected)
Continuing Dhurandhar: The Revenge theatrical run
Net debt down to Rs 365 Cr - balance-sheet de-risking re-rating
Cannes 2026 / Hollywood slate announcements; MAMI indie distribution pipeline
